summary: local-mcp performs install-time fetch and execution from non-publisher infrastructure with system data collection
details: The package ships three install/runtime scripts (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that combine child_process, fs, os, and https/http modules to fetch and execute remote content. download.js and index.js make outbound requests to https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — a Railway-hosted endpoint unrelated to the package's nominal publisher domains (local-mcp.com, download.local-mcp.com) — and read os.homedir() and process.env alongside the network calls. setup.js contains multiple POST request paths combined with child_process invocation. The Railway endpoint is an anonymous hosting target (free-tier subdomain, not a versioned CDN, no publisher attribution), and the install pipeline writes to disk and spawns processes based on the response. This matches the install-time dropper / system-info exfiltration shape: native fetch + filesystem write + process spawn + collection of host identifiers, with the C2 destination hosted on infrastructure that does not match the package's stated publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
